file_name: SPIENERGYCO,LTD_07_10_2014-EX-10-Cooperation Agreement of 50MWp Photovoltaic Grid-connected Power Generation Project in Yangqiao of~1.txt

perturbation.original_text: Party B promises that land nature will not be changed for building large-scale PV grid-connected power generation project in the plot; comprehensive development will be carried out according to relevant national stipulations, such commercial crops will be interplanted as agriculture and forestry as well as medicinal materials; local employees will enjoy the priority to be employed; local building materials will be adopted and relevant expenses will be paid on schedule.
perturbation.changed_text: Party B should endeavor to avoid changing the land nature for building the PV project; development may be carried out with consideration for national stipulations, potentially interplanting commercial crops; preference should be given to local employees when possible; the adoption of local building materials is encouraged, and relevant expenses will be addressed.
perturbation.explanation: This change significantly weakens the obligations of Party B regarding land use, development, employment, and expenses. Replacing 'promises that land nature will not be changed' with 'should endeavor to avoid changing the land nature' makes the obligation less strict. Similarly, changing 'will be carried out' to 'may be carried out with consideration' makes comprehensive development optional. Replacing 'will enjoy the priority to be employed' with 'preference should be given to local employees when possible' weakens the commitment to local hiring. Substituting 'will be adopted' with 'is encouraged' makes using local building materials a suggestion rather than a requirement. Finally, 'will be paid on schedule' is replaced with 'will be addressed,' which is vague and offers no assurance of timely payment. These changes create ambiguity and weaken the legally binding nature of these provisions.
perturbation.contradicted_law: The modified text could contradict regulations related to land use and fair labor practices in China, specifically those that ensure agricultural land preservation and prioritize local employment in development projects.
perturbation.law_citation: Land Administration Law of the People's Republic of China, Article 4
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 4 states "State protects and develops land resources, strictly implements the basic national policy of cherishing and rationally using every inch of land and practically protecting cultivated land." The change to 'should endeavor to avoid changing' contradicts the strict implementation of land protection by making it only an effort.
perturbation.justification: The original text imposes strict obligations on Party B regarding land use, development, employment, and expenses. The changed text weakens these obligations by replacing mandatory terms with discretionary or suggestive language. The Law Explanation references Article 4, which states that the State 'strictly implements the basic national policy of cherishing and rationally using every inch of land and practically protecting cultivated land.' The change from 'promises that land nature will not be changed' to 'should endeavor to avoid changing' directly contradicts the strict implementation of land protection described in the Law Explanation, creating ambiguity and weakening the legally binding nature of the provisions. This falls under the Ambiguity Legal Contradiction definition.
